title: Chicken, Rice, and Broccoli Bowl
summary: A high-protein staple bowl that uses common pantry basics and is easy to batch cook.
substitutions: Swap chicken for tofu, turkey, or canned tuna.
Swap broccoli for green beans, spinach, or frozen mixed vegetables.
instructions: Cook the rice according to package directions.
Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and garlic if available, then cook until browned and fully done.
Steam or saute the broccoli until tender-crisp.
Assemble the bowl and finish with soy sauce or lemon if available.
nutrition_summary: Protein-forward with enough carbohydrates to support training and recovery.
confidence_note: Strong fit when you want a simple, repeatable meal with minimal friction.

title: Greek Yogurt Oats Bowl
summary: A fast breakfast or snack built around protein, fiber, and easy pantry ingredients.
substitutions: Swap Greek yogurt for cottage cheese or skyr.
Swap oats for granola or overnight oats.
instructions: Add Greek yogurt to a bowl.
Stir in oats and let them sit briefly if you want a softer texture.
Top with fruit and peanut butter if available.
Adjust sweetness with a small amount of honey if needed.
nutrition_summary: High protein with moderate carbs and strong satiety for a lighter-calorie meal.
confidence_note: Strong fit for quick mornings and appetite control.

title: Egg and Veggie Skillet
summary: A fast skillet meal that turns leftover vegetables into a protein-first breakfast or lunch.
substitutions: Swap spinach for kale, peppers, or any quick-cooking vegetable.
Add egg whites if you want more protein with less fat.
instructions: Saute the vegetables in a pan with a small amount of oil if available.
Add beaten eggs and cook gently until just set.
Finish with cheese if you want extra flavor and calories.
Serve on its own or with toast if you need more carbohydrates.
nutrition_summary: Protein-forward and lower calorie density, with easy room to scale carbs up or down.
confidence_note: Strong fit when vegetables need to be used quickly.

title: Salmon, Potato, and Spinach Plate
summary: A recovery-friendly dinner with protein, carbs, and micronutrient-rich greens.
substitutions: Swap salmon for white fish, chicken, or tofu.
Swap potato for rice or quinoa.
instructions: Roast or pan-cook the potatoes until tender.
Cook the salmon until it flakes easily.
Wilt the spinach with garlic if available.
Plate everything together and finish with lemon or yogurt sauce if available.
nutrition_summary: Balanced meal with quality protein and recovery-supportive carbohydrates.
confidence_note: Strong fit for dinner after a harder training session.

title: Turkey and Bean Chili
summary: A hearty one-pot meal that scales well for meal prep and recovery days.
substitutions: Swap ground turkey for lean beef, chicken, or lentils.
Swap beans for chickpeas or black beans depending on what you have.
instructions: Brown the ground turkey with onion and garlic if available.
Add tomato, beans, and chopped vegetables.
Simmer until the flavors come together and the chili thickens.
Serve alone or over rice if you want a larger meal.
nutrition_summary: High-protein and filling, with easy portion scaling for bigger calorie needs.
confidence_note: Strong fit for batch cooking and multi-day meal prep.

title: Tofu Veggie Stir-Fry
summary: A flexible skillet meal that works well for lighter eating or plant-forward meals.
substitutions: Swap tofu for chicken, tempeh, or shrimp.
Swap rice for noodles or cauliflower rice.
instructions: Press and cube the tofu, then brown it in a hot pan.
Cook the rice separately if it is not already made.
Stir-fry the vegetables until crisp-tender.
Combine everything with soy sauce, garlic, and ginger if available.
nutrition_summary: Lean, protein-aware, and easy to keep moderate in calories while still satisfying.
confidence_note: Strong fit when you want a lighter dinner with good pantry coverage.

title: Cottage Cheese Toast Plate
summary: A minimal-prep meal for days when speed matters more than culinary ambition.
substitutions: Swap cottage cheese for Greek yogurt or ricotta.
Swap bread for crackers, wraps, or rice cakes.
instructions: Toast the bread if desired.
Top with cottage cheese and sliced vegetables if available.
Add a cooked egg if you want more protein.
Season with pepper and a small amount of oil if available.
nutrition_summary: Quick, high satiety, and easy to portion for a lighter meal.
confidence_note: Decent fit when time and energy are both low.

title: Peanut Butter Banana Protein Smoothie
summary: A fast blender option for recovery or higher-calorie intake when appetite is low.
substitutions: Swap milk for soy milk or water plus yogurt.
Swap banana for frozen berries or dates.
instructions: Add all ingredients to a blender.
Blend until smooth, adding liquid to adjust thickness.
Use oats or yogurt if you want more body and calories.
Drink immediately or chill briefly.
nutrition_summary: Protein-rich and carb-supportive, useful when recovery speed and convenience matter.
confidence_note: Strong fit after training or when solid food is unappealing.
